text: In Aula 16 you shared results with the present perfect: we have reached our target.
Today you talk about experiences: I have visited many clients, and I have travelled abroad.

I have visited many clients abroad.
Last year, I went to a conference in London.
I have never worked with a difficult client.
Have you ever travelled abroad for work?
Recently, I have started a new project.
Hi, David. Have you ever travelled abroad for work?
Yes, I have visited clients in many countries. Last year, I went to a conference in London.
Nice! Have you ever negotiated a big contract?
Yes, I have negotiated many. Last month, I met a new client on a trip to Paris.
Wonderful. And what have you done recently?
Recently, I have started a new project. It is a great experience!
I have never worked abroad.

Good morning, Gleice. Let me tell you about my work experiences. I have visited many clients in different cities, and I have been abroad twice for big conferences. Last year, I went to a conference in London, and I presented our new product to two hundred people. I have negotiated many contracts, but the contract in London was the most important one. I have never worked with a difficult client, because I always prepare well. Recently, I have started a new project, and I am very excited about it. So, tell me, Gleice, what work experiences have you had this year?

Hello, my name is Renata, and I work in the commercial team. Let me share my experiences with you. I have travelled abroad many times for my job, and I have visited clients in five countries. Last month, I went to a conference in Paris, and I met many new partners there. I have negotiated some big contracts, and I have learned a lot from each trip. I have never missed an important meeting, because I plan everything carefully. Recently, I have joined a new project, and it is a great experience. These trips have helped me grow, and I am ready for the next one.

First, David shares a general experience with the present perfect: I have visited many clients. Then he gives a finished moment with the past simple: last year, I went to a conference in London. After that, he uses ever and never: I have never worked with a difficult client. Next, he talks about a recent experience: recently, I have started a new project. Finally, he asks about your experiences: what work experiences have you had this year?
